name: Podio
one_liner: Progress Podio is a customizable work-management and collaboration platform (often used as a low-code CRM) where teams build 'apps' of structured items in workspaces.
primary_auth: OAuth2 authorization-code (server-side) flow using a self-registered Client ID/Secret; Podio also offers client-side, username & password, and app-authentication OAuth2 flows
access_notes: Sign up for a free Podio account (free plan supports up to 5 employees, 100 items) and generate an API key (Client ID + Secret) self-serve from the account settings page; Free and Plus plans include ~1,000 API calls/hour.
api_notes: The entire Podio frontend is built on the public REST API and all of it is exposed: 41 documented areas (Items, Applications, Tasks, Files, Comments, Hooks, Organizations, Spaces, Search, etc.), roughly 150-250+ operations; official SDKs for PHP, .NET, Ruby, Java, Python, and mobile.
mcp_notes: Vendor-hosted MCP server at https://mcp.podio.com/mcp with 22 read/write tools (orgs, workspaces, apps, items, tasks, comments, notifications); OAuth2 login with shared test credentials (~30-40 requests) or your own API key for production.

evidence.claim: Podio API uses OAuth2 with four flows (server-side, client-side, username & password, app authentication); access tokens valid 8 hours, refresh tokens 28 days
API reference documents 41 resource areas covering essentially all Podio functionality; API keys are grabbed from the account settings page after accepting API terms
Podio pricing includes a $0 free plan (5 users, 100 items); Free and Plus plans include ~1,000 API calls per hour
Hooks API provides outbound webhooks with app-level (item create/update/delete, comments, files, forms, tags) and space-level events, with a URL verification handshake
Official vendor-maintained Podio MCP Server at https://mcp.podio.com/mcp with 22 tools, usable from Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, and Copilot Studio

open_questions: Exact total endpoint count is not published (estimated 150-250+ from 41 areas). Free plan's 100-item cap may constrain realistic integration testing, and long-term roadmap under Progress ownership was not assessed.
